title: Public validation baseline sync
description: Refresh public-facing validation claims so README and demo documents match the current shipped baseline instead of older pass counts.
implementation_tasks: Scan all public-facing docs for stale validation counts, stale dates, and outdated checkpoint wording.
Update README and any owner-facing guide that still references older pytest or browser baselines.
Keep the validation wording truthful: only claim counts that are part of the current validated baseline.
Avoid broad narrative rewrites unless a stale validation claim materially changes the meaning.
done_when: public docs no longer reference outdated validation counts like 168 or 15 where the current baseline is 169 and 16
the top-level README and main demo guide match the current validated baseline
public validation language is consistent across the primary owner-facing entrypoints

title: Queue-first demo path and route-map consistency pass
description: Align the public walkthroughs around the current strongest route through the product so the docs do not contradict the shipped UI path from queue to intake to fresh incident review.
implementation_tasks: Audit public demo docs for route order contradictions, especially any path that starts directly at `/inputs` instead of the current queue-first operator path.

Standardize the strongest truthful walkthrough as `queue -> seeded incident -> Inspect intake -> /inputs -> fresh nxs incident -> /training -> /settings` unless a doc explicitly explains an alternate path.
Clarify when a route is directly linked in the UI versus reached through another screen.
Keep seeded and fresh-incident guidance semantically aligned.
done_when: the primary public docs agree on the strongest review order and navigation path
a first-time owner can move through the UI without contradictory route instructions
public route guidance matches the shipped navigation model

title: Public product-story truth pass
description: Review public product and buyer docs for small truth drifts so the current five-family wedge, bounded runtime posture, and operator story read consistently across demo, buyer, and GTM materials.
implementation_tasks: Read the public strategy, buyer proof, presentation, visual-architecture, and setup materials end to end.
Correct wording that still reflects an older intermediate state or leaves ambiguity about what is real versus bounded.
Preserve the current product category: support-to-engineering investigation for recurring customer-facing outages.
Do not broaden the product promise beyond the shipped five-family wedge.
done_when: public materials describe the same current product state and boundaries
bounded REPLICA, bounded TRACE, runtime-backed versus inference-first posture, and governed approval are described consistently
buyer-facing material does not require oral correction during a demo

title: Internal doc and control-surface consistency pass
description: Bring the internal operating docs into the same truth set as the current wrapped baseline and the new documentation-sync frontier so future agents do not start from mixed signals.
implementation_tasks: Review internal indexes, checklists, and runbooks for stale baseline numbers or outdated phase/frontier references.
Keep control docs explicit about the current wrapped five-family baseline and the narrow documentation-sync follow-up phase.
Refresh any internal references that still imply an active implementation loop when there is only a planned backlog.
Keep the backlog references intentional and minimal.
done_when: internal docs and control docs describe the same current baseline and next frontier
there are no obvious stale pass-count or phase-marker contradictions in the main internal entrypoints
the next loop can start from one clear control surface

title: Documentation truth checkpoint and backlog close
description: Validate the documentation-sync phase end to end and close the backlog cleanly so the repo returns to one coherent truth surface.
implementation_tasks: Rerun the narrow validation gates after items 145-148 complete.
Refresh AGENTS, WORKING_STATE, and the loop runbook to mark the documentation-sync backlog complete.
Update internal and public index docs if the reading order or active references changed.
Leave the next frontier blank unless a new narrow backlog is intentionally opened.
done_when: control docs point to the new truth without stale active-backlog ambiguity
the repo has one coherent post-backlog documentation surface
